name: Camarillas Reservoir Loop from Agramón
name_original: Estación de Agramón al Embalse de Camarillas — ruta circular
description: Hike a moderate 10.3-mile (16.6 km) circular route from Estación de Agramón to Embalse de Camarillas, exploring riverbanks and reservoir vie
region_intro: Hiking around Hellín offers diverse natural landscapes, including the unique geological formations of the Cancarix Volcano and the river systems of the Mundo and Segura. The region features varied terrain, from volcanic escarpments and canyons to areas with lakes and archaeological sites. These natural features provide a range of hiking experiences across different difficulty levels. Best hiking trails around Hellín The most popular hiking route is Sendero del Volcán de Cancarix — circular en el Monumento Natural Pitón Volcánico de Cancarix, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique geological formations and vertical escarpments of the Cancarix Volcano. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pitón Volcánico de Cancarix — circular, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views around the volcanic monument, with an estimated completion time of 2 hours 41 minutes. Local hikers also love the Cañón de los Almadenes Circular Route, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through canyon landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Hellín is defined by volcanic landscapes, river valleys, and canyons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Hellín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Hellín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Hellín?
A: Hellín offers a diverse range of natural landscapes. You'll encounter the unique geological formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Cancarix Volcano&lt;/strong&gt;, which is a seven-million-year-old natural monument. The region also features picturesque river systems where the Mundo and Segura rivers converge, creating varied terrain including areas that resemble rice fields. Additionally, you can explore natural monuments, lakes, canyons, and caves.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Hellín area?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes available around Hellín, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes 12 easy routes, 24 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Hellín?
A: Yes, Hellín has several excellent circular routes. For instance, you can explore the volcanic landscape on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1066772870/sendero-del-volcan-de-cancarix-circular-en-el-monumento-natural-piton-volcanico-de-cancarix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Volcán de Cancarix&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110678655/piton-volcanico-de-cancarix-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitón Volcánico de Cancarix&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110918211/canon-de-los-almadenes-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almadenes Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through stunning canyon landscapes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on Hellín's trails?
A: Hikers can discover several unique natural features. The &lt;strong&gt;Cancarix Volcano&lt;/strong&gt; is a prominent geological marvel. You can also find the picturesque confluence of the Mundo and Segura rivers. Other attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110939665/laguna-de-los-patos-circular-desde-hellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Patos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt;, and the hidden natural pool known as 'Salto de Tarzán' in Agramón.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers a blend of natural and historical exploration. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento Arqueológico del Tolmo de Minateda&lt;/a&gt; is a significant archaeological park that was inhabited from the Bronze Age. This site also features UNESCO World Heritage cave paintings in the Abrigo Grande de Minateda.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Hellín?
A: The hiking routes in Hellín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 29 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from volcanic landscapes to river valleys and canyons, and appreciate the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies like lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. You can hike around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110939665/laguna-de-los-patos-circular-desde-hellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Patos&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.5 km circular route near Hellín. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119179020/camarillas-reservoir-loop-from-agramon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarillas Reservoir Loop from Agramón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer, moderate experience around the reservoir.

Q: What is the difficulty level of most hikes in Hellín?
A: Most hikes in Hellín are classified as moderate, with 24 out of 37 routes falling into this category. There are also 12 easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk, and one more challenging route for experienced hikers.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery on Hellín's trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer excellent viewpoints. For example, routes around the Cancarix Volcano, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110678655/piton-volcanico-de-cancarix-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitón Volcánico de Cancarix — circular&lt;/a&gt;, provide panoramic views from the summit. You can also find scenic vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serreta Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through canyons or gorges?
A: Yes, Hellín is known for its impressive canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110918211/canon-de-los-almadenes-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almadenes Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice for exploring these dramatic landscapes. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1644830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walkway of the Río Mundo Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting caves or rock formations to explore?
A: Hellín has several fascinating cave systems and rock formations. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eremitorio Rupestre de Alborajico&lt;/a&gt;, which is a cave hermitage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serreta Cave&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the region's geological wonders.
tips: The Agramón Station is a starting point to visit nearby tourist places such as the Sierra del Segura and the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park.

The name of the Tarzan Leap comes from the fact that, according to legend, the actor Johnny Weissmuller, famous for playing Tarzan in the movies, visited this place in the 1950s and jumped from the waterfall. It is a natural pool formed by the waterfall of the Arroyo de la Hoz, which falls from a height of about 6 meters. The place has picnic areas and barbecues.

Its construction was completed in 1964 and it is used mainly for crop irrigation, although it is also used for hydroelectric power generation. In addition, it is a place where you can do activities such as fishing, hiking and bird watching.

faqs.question: Where does the Estación de Agramón to Embalse de Camarillas circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near the old railway station of Agramón. You should find parking available in the vicinity of the Agramón Station, which serves as the primary trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly recommended for this trail, as the deciduous trees along the Mundo River create a vibrant display of colors. The region generally has a local steppe microclimate with minimal precipitation, making it suitable for hiking during many seasons, but autumn offers exceptional scenery.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a moderate hike, spanning about 16.6 kilometers with around 90 meters of elevation gain. While the paths are generally accessible, a good level of physical fitness is recommended due to its length. It might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners, but experienced families could enjoy it.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Estación de Agramón to Embalse de Camarillas circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including paths and forest tracks. You'll walk through lush wooded areas with poplars, tamarisks, and ash trees, closely following the Mundo River. Highlights include the Salto de Tarzán, the thermal waters of Azaraque pond, and the expansive Camarillas Reservoir. The surrounding area also boasts fertile valleys with olive and almond groves.

faqs.question: What notable features or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty of the Camarillas Reservoir and the Salto de Tarzán, you'll pass the historic Agramón Station. The route also offers glimpses of archaeological sites and the unique volcanic piton in the Sierra de las Cabras. Don't miss the "Jardín Fluvial del Puente de Agramón" for a relaxing stop.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead or along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Agramón, which is a small town. While the trail itself is primarily natural, you may find basic amenities in Agramón. For more extensive options regarding cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you might need to look in nearby larger towns.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: Hikers generally complete this 16.6-kilometer route in approximately 4 hours and 18 minutes. However, this can vary based on your pace, the number of stops you make, and your fitness level.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate length and potential exposure, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach the trailhead in Agramón?
faqs.answer: While the trail starts near the old railway station, it is disused. Public transport options directly to the Agramón Station might be limited. It's best to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the trailhead.
